target.title: feat(voice-call): msteams — governance hardening + CVI extensions (echo fix, replay guard, retroactive vision)
target.body:
> **Stacked on #91438 — review just the last 18 commits** (`5249ad9bbb…2110577317`): **13 files, +1,277/−227** (478 of the additions are tests). The full diff includes #91438's commits only until that PR lands; this branch then rebases to exactly the increment. A commit→feature→tests map is below.

## Summary

Fixes the live realtime **self-answer (echo) bug**, makes the bridge handshake **replay-proof**, and makes group-call etiquette and **verbal interrupts deterministic** (enforced in code at the audio egress, not model-trusted). On top of that: **retroactive vision** ("what did the earlier slide say?"), **per-thread sessions**, **captioned/slideshow visual sharing**, and a **"thinking" avatar expression**.

Three layers at a glance:

| Layer | Themes |
| --- | --- |
| **Correctness / security** | echo self-answer fix (`suppressInputDuringPlayback` default → on), replay-proof HMAC tuples, deterministic group gate on realtime, playout clock for echo + notify drain |
| **Call UX** | verbal interrupts ("stop", wake-phrase forms), notify call-backs (deliver → hang up, un-clipped) |
| **CVI** | retroactive vision (16-keyframe ring), scene-change + camera/screen ambient vision, captions + slideshow, "thinking" expression, per-thread sessions |

## ⚠️ Behavior changes

- `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` default **flipped off → on**: default-off *was* the live self-answer bug — the bot answered its own echo on every realtime deployment. Set `false` to restore the old behavior; tuning via `realtime.echoSuppressionWindowMs` / `realtime.echoBargeInRms`.
- Outbound realtime call-backs now honor `outbound.defaultMode` (default **notify** = state the result, then hang up after the audio drains). Previously they always lingered conversationally; set `"conversation"` to keep that.

## 🛡️ Security & correctness hardening

- **Single-use HMAC upgrade tuples (replay guard)** — a captured bridge handshake replayed within the HMAC window could open a ghost session once the live call ended (duplicate-callId only protects while connected). Verified `(callId, timestamp, signature)` tuples are now single-use; a legitimate reconnect carries a fresh timestamp; only verified tuples are recorded (no unauthenticated map growth) and entries expire with the window. No worker change. Tested: connect OK → exact replay 401 → fresh-timestamp reconnect OK.
- **Deterministic group-call gate (realtime)** — "speak only when addressed" was instruction-only on the realtime path. Every finished caller turn is now evaluated with the same `shouldRespondToGroupTurn` core the streaming path uses, and an unaddressed meeting turn's reply **audio is dropped at the egress** — etiquette holds even if the model ignores instructions. Participant count wired live; 1:1 never gates.
- **Echo guard on a playout clock** — the realtime model streams audio faster than realtime, so last-send time is not play-time; the guard and the notify drain both key on a playout estimate (accumulated chunk durations, collapsed on barge-in), so they hold for the whole spoken reply. Regression test: burst 10 s of audio → echo still suppressed at +2 s.

## 📞 Call experience

- **Deterministic verbal interrupts** — "stop", "wait", "hold on", "never mind" handled in code (the model is mid-generation when they arrive): streaming cuts playback **and suppresses the reply**; realtime flushes the worker queue immediately. Whole-utterance matching with filler **and configured wake-phrase stripping** — *"⟨name⟩, stop"* cuts as instantly as bare *"stop"*; *"stop by the store"* never triggers.
- **Notify call-backs deliver-and-hang-up without clipping** — drain-aware (playout clock) + `notifyHangupDelaySec` grace.

## 🎥 CVI extensions

- **Retroactive vision (frame history)** — a ring of 16 scene-change keyframes per call; `look_at_screen scope:"history"` attaches up to 6 timestamped, participant-attributed keyframes to **one budgeted consult** — "what did the earlier slide say?", "catch me up on what was shown". No eager OCR-per-keyframe (the model reads at question time; same `maxVisionPerMinute` philosophy).
- **Scene-change-driven ambient vision** — frames reach the realtime model on arrival (was up to ~6 s late); the poll remains as a backstop. **Camera + screen simultaneously**, per-source dedup + labels.
- **`show_to_caller` captions + slideshow** — caption strip on the tile; multi-image results play as a paced slideshow (previously only the last image was visible). Additive protocol; older renderers ignore both.
- **"Thinking" avatar expression** while consult / `look_at_screen` / `show_to_caller` runs; the reply re-cues the real emotion.
- **Per-thread sessions** — `sessionScope: "per-thread"` keys the agent session by the Teams meeting/chat thread on both paths; parallel conversations stay isolated, recurring meetings keep context. PSTN falls through to per-phone; events carry an additive optional `threadId`.

## Commit → feature → tests map (the 18-commit increment)

| Feature | Commits | Tests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Echo / self-answer fix (+ playout clock, streaming path, knobs) | `cc9f7302b3`, `64aca022c3`, `514dffba49` | pcm16Rms units; suppress-vs-barge-in; burst-playout regression |
| Replay guard | `1bd04c1417` | connect → replay 401 → fresh reconnect |
| Deterministic group gate | `6676bdf3da` | unaddressed audio dropped; addressed flows; 1:1 ungated |
| Verbal interrupts (+ wake-phrase form) | `5c827481f7`, `2110577317` | matcher units (positives/negatives/name-forms); realtime flush |
| Retroactive vision | `0af72f4f8e`, `6676bdf3da` | history ring; history-scope look attaches keyframes |
| Per-thread sessions | `d1733c2d95` | session-key resolution |
| Captions + slideshow | `f4565c05d5`, `72fd39cec7` | toTileCaption units; display path |
| Notify call-backs (mode + drain) | `41c2d3c11e`, `0b28bf2d0b` | delivery-complete after drain; once-only |
| Scene-change + camera/screen vision | `7cf6b04582`, `5b7f3db8af` | event-driven push dedup; both-sources push |
| "Thinking" expression | `d5c2a0fa76` | cue on tool start; neutral re-cue |
| Refactors (vision store, tts playback) | `5249ad9bbb`, `7654aaacb5` | existing suites green (no behavior change) |

## Foundation recap (from #91438, included in this diff until it lands)

The `msteams` provider: inbound Teams voice + video vision (`look_at_screen`, streaming auto-attach, realtime continuous vision via `RealtimeVoiceBridge.sendImage`), group/meeting awareness (per-participant attribution + speak-only-when-addressed), outbound "call me back" with voicemail fallback, avatar rendering drivers (expression cues, viseme lip-shape, `show_to_caller`), caller allowlist (closed by default), recording-status gate on every media-derived path, HMAC-authenticated bridge WebSocket, SSRF-guarded outbound. Live-proven on 1:1 and 3-party group Teams calls (see #91438's proof).

## Config / compatibility notes

- New optional keys: `realtime.echoSuppressionWindowMs`, `realtime.echoBargeInRms`; new `sessionScope` value `"per-thread"`; new optional event field `threadId` (additive).
- All bridge protocol additions are additive (`display.image.caption`, slideshow `durationMs`); an older worker ignores them. The replay guard requires no worker change.
- The recording-status gate (Media Access API) covers every media-derived path the new features touch.

## Relationship to #91438

**Depends on #91438** (the msteams provider foundation) — stacked, not superseding; the shared history disappears from this diff when it lands. If maintainers prefer, the **echo-guard + replay-guard fixes can be extracted into a standalone fast-track PR** — they're independent of the vision/display features.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The realtime self-answer (echo) bug observed on live Teams calls, bridge handshake replay, non-deterministic group gating on realtime, notify call-back clipping, and the verbal-interrupt / retroactive-vision / per-thread-session / visual-sharing features above.
- **Real environment tested:** Foundation behavior was live-proven on real 1:1 and 3-party group Microsoft Teams calls in #91438 (see its proof + user-attachment evidence). This increment is verified by the full local gates on this branch: oxlint, `tsgo` prod types, `check:test-types`, and the voice-call suites — 114 tests including new regression tests per the map above. Live re-verification of the increment on a real Teams call is scheduled and will be added (one line of live evidence per claim: echo no longer self-answers; "stop" cut latency; history-scope answer about an earlier slide; the replay-rejection 401 log line) before this PR leaves draft.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm tsgo:prod && pnpm check:test-types && pnpm test extensions/voice-call` (all green). Live (pending, will update): place a Teams call on this build; confirm no self-answer + spoken barge-in still works; "stop" mid-reply cuts instantly; group call stays silent until addressed by name; "what did the earlier slide say?" answers from keyframes; `show_to_caller` renders a captioned image; an `openclaw_agent_task` "call me back" delivers and hangs up un-clipped.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  Test Files  5 passed (5)
       Tests  114 passed  (voice-call: provider / realtime / media-stream / verbal-interrupt / config)
  tsgo: 0 errors · check:test-types: 0 errors · oxlint: 0 errors
  ```
  Foundation live evidence: #91438 (screenshots/video + redacted transcripts).
- **Observed result after fix:** All gates green at every commit; regression tests reproduce the original echo/replay/clipping failure modes and confirm the fixes (burst-generated audio: echo still suppressed at +2 s where the old last-send window had expired; exact handshake replay → 401).
- **What was not tested:** Live re-verification of this increment on a real Teams call (scheduled; unit/regression-tested only — foundation behavior was live-proven in #91438). Google realtime provider (`sendImage` is an optional method → safe no-op). Azure Speech visemes (this stack runs ElevenLabs + realtime → RMS + estimated visemes). Compliance-recording tenant policies beyond the recording-status gate.
